{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the overdetermined linear system $Ax=b$, where $A$ has $m$ rows and $n$ columns with $m>n$. Suppose that applying Gaussian elimination with complete pivoting to $A$ yields\n\\[\nPAQ=L\\begin{bmatrix}U_{11}&U_{12}\\\\0&0\\end{bmatrix},\n\\]\nwhere $P$ and $Q$ are permutation matrices, $L$ is an $m\\times m$ lower triangular matrix, $U_{11}$ is a $\\bar m\\times \\bar m$ upper triangular nonsingular matrix, $U_{12}$ is a $\\bar m\\times (n-\\bar m)$ matrix, and $\\bar m\\le n$ is the rank of $A$. When $\\bar m=n$, find the unique solution of $Ax=b$."}
